input.headline: Why are my health insurance premiums going up so much?

raw.headline: Why are my health insurance premiums going up so much?

winner: My health insurance premiums went down so much, now I have to pay for a doctor's advice—it's just cheaper to ask Google and save my co-pay!

candidates.text: Because my doctor said I'm basically a walking risk factor now.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on health insurance premiums with a touch of dark humor."}

candidates.text: I told my insurance company I want to pay more, and they said "Sure, let's put a premium on your premium!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Playful wordplay with 'premium' and 'put a premium on your premium,' showing creativity and relevance to the topic."}

candidates.text: Because my doctor keeps upgrading my plan to platinum, and I haven't even told him I'm allergic to copper.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a good punchline involving an allergy to copper, which ties humorously into 'platinum' upgrades. It's concise and surprising, though the connection between insurance plans and metal allergies could be slightly more grounded for maximum clarity."}

candidates.text: My health insurance premiums went down so much, now I have to pay for a doctor's advice—it's just cheaper to ask Google and save my co-pay!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny with a clever twist comparing Google to a doctor, but it doesn't directly address the headline's question and feels slightly off-topic."}

candidates.text: I'm just glad they're not charging me extra for breathing—they might start doing that next year.
